---
name: project-gin-framework
description: "Overview of the gin HTTP framework project — architecture, core types, and request lifecycle"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 4819cd2f-8c00-4d16-8488-77475172230f
---

This is the [gin-gonic/gin](https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin) Go HTTP framework repository.

**Core types and locations:**
- `Engine` — gin.go:90, embeds RouterGroup, owns the radix tree forest and Context pool
- `RouterGroup` — routergroup.go:55, hierarchical route grouping with shared middleware/path prefix
- `Context` — context.go:59, per-request state; pooled via sync.Pool
- `HandlersChain` — gin.go:57, `[]HandlerFunc` — middleware + handler chain
- `node` (radix tree) — tree.go:99, supports static/param/catchAll node types

**Request lifecycle entry point:** `Engine.ServeHTTP` at gin.go:662

**Why:** Understanding architecture to support codebase navigation and feature work.
**How to apply:** Reference these locations when answering questions about routing, middleware, or request handling.

1	// Copyright 2014 Manu Martinez-Almeida. All rights reserved.
2	// Use of this source code is governed by a MIT style
3	// license that can be found in the LICENSE file.
4	
5	package gin
6	
7	import (
8		"fmt"
9		"html/template"
10		"net"
11		"net/http"
12		"os"
13		"path"
14		"strings"
15		"sync"
16	
17		"github.com/gin-gonic/gin/internal/bytesconv"
18		filesystem "github.com/gin-gonic/gin/internal/fs"
19		"github.com/gin-gonic/gin/render"
20		"github.com/quic-go/quic-go/http3"
21		"golang.org/x/net/http2"
22		"golang.org/x/net/http2/h2c"
23	)
24	
25	const (
26		defaultMultipartMemory = 32 << 20 // 32 MB
27		escapedColon           = "\\:"
28		colon                  = ":"
29		backslash              = "\\"
30	)
31	
32	var (
33		default404Body = []byte("404 page not found")
34		default405Body = []byte("405 method not allowed")
35	)
36	
37	var defaultPlatform string
38	
39	var defaultTrustedCIDRs = []*net.IPNet{
40		{ // 0.0.0.0/0 (IPv4)
41			IP:   net.IP{0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0},
42			Mask: net.IPMask{0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0},
43		},
44		{ // ::/0 (IPv6)
45			IP:   net.IP{0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0},
46			Mask: net.IPMask{0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0},
47		},
48	}
49	
50	// HandlerFunc defines the handler used by gin middleware as return value.
51	type HandlerFunc func(*Context)
52	
53	// OptionFunc defines the function to change the default configuration
54	type OptionFunc func(*Engine)
55	
56	// HandlersChain defines a HandlerFunc slice.
57	type HandlersChain []HandlerFunc
58	
59	// Last returns the last handler in the chain. i.e. the last handler is the main one.
60	func (c HandlersChain) Last() HandlerFunc {
61		if length := len(c); length > 0 {
62			return c[length-1]
63		}
64		return nil
65	}
66	
67	// RouteInfo represents a request route's specification which contains method and path and its handler.
68	type RouteInfo struct {
69		Method      string
70		Path        string
71		Handler     string
72		HandlerFunc HandlerFunc
73	}
74	
75	// RoutesInfo defines a RouteInfo slice.
76	type RoutesInfo []RouteInfo
77	
78	// Trusted platforms
79	const (
80		// PlatformGoogleAppEngine when running on Google App Engine. Trust X-Appengine-Remote-Addr
81		// for determining the client's IP
82		PlatformGoogleAppEngine = "X-Appengine-Remote-Addr"
83		// PlatformCloudflare when using Cloudflare's CDN. Trust CF-Connecting-IP for determining
84		// the client's IP
85		PlatformCloudflare = "CF-Connecting-IP"
86		// PlatformFlyIO when running on Fly.io. Trust Fly-Client-IP for determining the client's IP
87		PlatformFlyIO = "Fly-Client-IP"
88	)
89	
90	// Engine is the framework's instance, it contains the muxer, middleware and configuration settings.
91	// Create an instance of Engine, by using New() or Default()
92	type Engine struct {
93		RouterGroup
94	
95		// routeTreesUpdated ensures that the initialization or update of the route trees
96		// (used for routing HTTP requests) happens only once, even if called multiple times concurrently.
97		routeTreesUpdated sync.Once
98	
99		// RedirectTrailingSlash enables automatic redirection if the current route can't be matched but a
100		// handler for the path with (without) the trailing slash exists.
101		// For example if /foo/ is requested but a route only exists for /foo, the
102		// client is redirected to /foo with http status code 301 for GET requests
103		// and 307 for all other request methods.
104		RedirectTrailingSlash bool
105	
106		// RedirectFixedPath if enabled, the router tries to fix the current request path, if no
107		// handle is registered for it.
108		// First superfluous path elements like ../ or // are removed.
109		// Afterwards the router does a case-insensitive lookup of the cleaned path.
110		// If a handle can be found for this route, the router makes a redirection
111		// to the corrected path with status code 301 for GET requests and 307 for
112		// all other request methods.
113		// For example /FOO and /..//Foo could be redirected to /foo.
114		// RedirectTrailingSlash is independent of this option.
115		RedirectFixedPath bool
116	
117		// HandleMethodNotAllowed if enabled, the router checks if another method is allowed for the
118		// current route, if the current request can not be routed.
119		// If this is the case, the request is answered with 'Method Not Allowed'
120		// and HTTP status code 405.
121		// If no other Method is allowed, the request is delegated to the NotFound
122		// handler.
123		HandleMethodNotAllowed bool
124	
125		// ForwardedByClientIP if enabled, client IP will be parsed from the request's headers that
126		// match those stored at `(*gin.Engine).RemoteIPHeaders`. If no IP was
127		// fetched, it falls back to the IP obtained from
128		// `(*gin.Context).Request.RemoteAddr`.
129		ForwardedByClientIP bool
130	
131		// AppEngine was deprecated.
132		// Deprecated: USE `TrustedPlatform` WITH VALUE `gin.PlatformGoogleAppEngine` INSTEAD
133		// #726 #755 If enabled, it will trust some headers starting with
134		// 'X-AppEngine...' for better integration with that PaaS.
135		AppEngine bool
136	
137		// UseRawPath if enabled, the url.RawPath will be used to find parameters.
138		// The RawPath is only a hint, EscapedPath() should be use instead. (https://pkg.go.dev/net/url@master#URL)
139		// Only use RawPath if you know what you are doing.
140		UseRawPath bool
141	
142		// UseEscapedPath if enable, the url.EscapedPath() will be used to find parameters
143		// It overrides UseRawPath
144		UseEscapedPath bool
145	
146		// UnescapePathValues if true, the path value will be unescaped.
147		// If UseRawPath and UseEscapedPath are false (by default), the UnescapePathValues effectively is true,
148		// as url.Path gonna be used, which is already unescaped.
149		UnescapePathValues bool
150	
151		// RemoveExtraSlash a parameter can be parsed from the URL even with extra slashes.
152		// See the PR #1817 and issue #1644
153		RemoveExtraSlash bool
154	
155		// RemoteIPHeaders list of headers used to obtain the client IP when
156		// `(*gin.Engine).ForwardedByClientIP` is `true` and
157		// `(*gin.Context).Request.RemoteAddr` is matched by at least one of the
158		// network origins of list defined by `(*gin.Engine).SetTrustedProxies()`.
159		RemoteIPHeaders []string
160	
161		// TrustedPlatform if set to a constant of value gin.Platform*, trusts the headers set by
162		// that platform, for example to determine the client IP
163		TrustedPlatform string
164	
165		// MaxMultipartMemory value of 'maxMemory' param that is given to http.Request's ParseMultipartForm
166		// method call.
167		MaxMultipartMemory int64
168	
169		// UseH2C enable h2c support.
170		UseH2C bool
171	
172		// ContextWithFallback enable fallback Context.Deadline(), Context.Done(), Context.Err() and Context.Value() when Context.Request.Context() is not nil.
173		ContextWithFallback bool
174	
175		delims           render.Delims
176		secureJSONPrefix string
177		HTMLRender       render.HTMLRender
178		FuncMap          template.FuncMap
179		allNoRoute       HandlersChain
180		allNoMethod      HandlersChain
181		noRoute          HandlersChain
182		noMethod         HandlersChain
183		pool             sync.Pool
184		trees            methodTrees
185		maxParams        uint16
186		maxSections      uint16
187		trustedProxies   []string
188		trustedCIDRs     []*net.IPNet
189	}
190	
191	var _ IRouter = (*Engine)(nil)
192	
193	// New returns a new blank Engine instance without any middleware attached.
194	// By default, the configuration is:
195	// - RedirectTrailingSlash:  true
196	// - RedirectFixedPath:      false
197	// - HandleMethodNotAllowed: false

364	func (engine *Engine) addRoute(method, path string, handlers HandlersChain) {
365		assert1(path[0] == '/', "path must begin with '/'")
366		assert1(method != "", "HTTP method can not be empty")
367		assert1(len(handlers) > 0, "there must be at least one handler")
368	
369		debugPrintRoute(method, path, handlers)
370	
371		root := engine.trees.get(method)
372		if root == nil {
373			root = new(node)
374			root.fullPath = "/"
375			engine.trees = append(engine.trees, methodTree{method: method, root: root})
376		}
377		root.addRoute(path, handlers)
378	
379		if paramsCount := countParams(path); paramsCount > engine.maxParams {
380			engine.maxParams = paramsCount
381		}
382	
383		if sectionsCount := countSections(path); sectionsCount > engine.maxSections {
384			engine.maxSections = sectionsCount
385		}
386	}

388	// Routes returns a slice of registered routes, including some useful information, such as:
389	// the http method, path, and the handler name.
390	func (engine *Engine) Routes() (routes RoutesInfo) {
391		for _, tree := range engine.trees {
392			routes = iterate("", tree.method, routes, tree.root)
393		}
394		return routes

397	func iterate(path, method string, routes RoutesInfo, root *node) RoutesInfo {
398		path += root.path
399		if len(root.handlers) > 0 {
400			handlerFunc := root.handlers.Last()
401			routes = append(routes, RouteInfo{
402				Method:      method,
403				Path:        path,
404				Handler:     nameOfFunction(handlerFunc),
405				HandlerFunc: handlerFunc,
406			})
407		}
408		for _, child := range root.children {
409			routes = iterate(path, method, routes, child)
410		}
411		return routes
412	}

414	func (engine *Engine) prepareTrustedCIDRs() ([]*net.IPNet, error) {
415		if engine.trustedProxies == nil {
416			return nil, nil
417		}
418	
419		cidr := make([]*net.IPNet, 0, len(engine.trustedProxies))
420		for _, trustedProxy := range engine.trustedProxies {
421			if !strings.Contains(trustedProxy, "/") {
422				ip := parseIP(trustedProxy)
423				if ip == nil {
424					return cidr, &net.ParseError{Type: "IP address", Text: trustedProxy}
425				}
426	
427				switch len(ip) {
428				case net.IPv4len:
429					trustedProxy += "/32"
430				case net.IPv6len:
431					trustedProxy += "/128"
432				}
433			}
434			_, cidrNet, err := net.ParseCIDR(trustedProxy)
435			if err != nil {
436				return cidr, err
437			}
438			cidr = append(cidr, cidrNet)
439		}
440		return cidr, nil
441	}

443	// SetTrustedProxies set a list of network origins (IPv4 addresses,
444	// IPv4 CIDRs, IPv6 addresses or IPv6 CIDRs) from which to trust
445	// request's headers that contain alternative client IP when
446	// `(*gin.Engine).ForwardedByClientIP` is `true`. `TrustedProxies`
447	// feature is enabled by default, and it also trusts all proxies
448	// by default. If you want to disable this feature, use
449	// Engine.SetTrustedProxies(nil), then Context.ClientIP() will
450	// return the remote address directly.

457	func (engine *Engine) isUnsafeTrustedProxies() bool {

461	// parseTrustedProxies parse Engine.trustedProxies to Engine.trustedCIDRs

468	// isTrustedProxy will check whether the IP address is included in the trusted list according to Engine.trustedCIDRs
469	func (engine *Engine) isTrustedProxy(ip net.IP) bool {
470		if engine.trustedCIDRs == nil {
471			return false
472		}
473		for _, cidr := range engine.trustedCIDRs {
474			if cidr.Contains(ip) {
475				return true
476			}
477		}
478		return false
479	}

481	// validateHeader will parse X-Forwarded-For header and return the trusted client IP address
482	func (engine *Engine) validateHeader(header string) (clientIP string, valid bool) {
483		if header == "" {
484			return "", false
485		}
486		items := strings.Split(header, ",")
487		for i := len(items) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
488			ipStr := strings.TrimSpace(items[i])
489			ip := net.ParseIP(ipStr)
490			if ip == nil {
491				break
492			}
493	
494			// X-Forwarded-For is appended by proxy
495			// Check IPs in reverse order and stop when find untrusted proxy
496			if (i == 0) || (!engine.isTrustedProxy(ip)) {
497				return ipStr, true
498			}
499		}
500		return "", false
501	}

503	// updateRouteTree do update to the route tree recursively
504	func updateRouteTree(n *node) {
505		n.path = strings.ReplaceAll(n.path, escapedColon, colon)
506		n.fullPath = strings.ReplaceAll(n.fullPath, escapedColon, colon)
507		n.indices = strings.ReplaceAll(n.indices, backslash, colon)
508		if n.children == nil {
509			return
510		}
511		for _, child := range n.children {
512			updateRouteTree(child)
513		}
514	}

516	// updateRouteTrees do update to the route trees
517	func (engine *Engine) updateRouteTrees() {
518		for _, tree := range engine.trees {
519			updateRouteTree(tree.root)
520		}
521	}

523	// parseIP parse a string representation of an IP and returns a net.IP with the
524	// minimum byte representation or nil if input is invalid.
525	func parseIP(ip string) net.IP {
526		parsedIP := net.ParseIP(ip)
527	
528		if ipv4 := parsedIP.To4(); ipv4 != nil {
529			// return ip in a 4-byte representation
530			return ipv4
531		}
532	
533		// return ip in a 16-byte representation or nil
534		return parsedIP
535	}

661	// ServeHTTP conforms to the http.Handler interface.
662	func (engine *Engine) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
663		engine.routeTreesUpdated.Do(func() {
664			engine.updateRouteTrees()
665		})
666	
667		c := engine.pool.Get().(*Context)
668		c.writermem.reset(w)
669		c.Request = req
670		c.reset()
671	
672		engine.handleHTTPRequest(c)
673	
674		engine.pool.Put(c)
675	}

677	// HandleContext re-enters a context that has been rewritten.
678	// This can be done by setting c.Request.URL.Path to your new target.
679	// Disclaimer: You can loop yourself to deal with this, use wisely.
680	func (engine *Engine) HandleContext(c *Context) {
681		oldIndexValue := c.index
682		oldHandlers := c.handlers
683		c.reset()
684		engine.handleHTTPRequest(c)
685	
686		c.index = oldIndexValue
687		c.handlers = oldHandlers
688	}
689	
690	func (engine *Engine) handleHTTPRequest(c *Context) {
691		httpMethod := c.Request.Method
692		rPath := c.Request.URL.Path
693		unescape := false
694	
695		if engine.UseEscapedPath {
696			rPath = c.Request.URL.EscapedPath()
697			unescape = engine.UnescapePathValues
698		} else if engine.UseRawPath && len(c.Request.URL.RawPath) > 0 {
699			rPath = c.Request.URL.RawPath
700			unescape = engine.UnescapePathValues
701		}
702	
703		if engine.RemoveExtraSlash {
704			rPath = cleanPath(rPath)
705		}
706	
707		// Find root of the tree for the given HTTP method
708		t := engine.trees
709		for i, tl := 0, len(t); i < tl; i++ {
710			if t[i].method != httpMethod {
711				continue
712			}
713			root := t[i].root
714			// Find route in tree
715			value := root.getValue(rPath, c.params, c.skippedNodes, unescape)
716			if value.params != nil {
717				c.Params = *value.params
718			}
719			if value.handlers != nil {
720				c.handlers = value.handlers
721				c.fullPath = value.fullPath
722				c.Next()
723				c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
724				return
725			}
726			if httpMethod != http.MethodConnect && rPath != "/" {
727				if value.tsr && engine.RedirectTrailingSlash {
728					redirectTrailingSlash(c)
729					return
730				}
731				if engine.RedirectFixedPath && redirectFixedPath(c, root, engine.RedirectFixedPath) {
732					return
733				}
734			}
735			break
736		}
737	
738		if engine.HandleMethodNotAllowed && len(t) > 0 {
739			// According to RFC 7231 section 6.5.5, MUST generate an Allow header field in response
740			// containing a list of the target resource's currently supported methods.
741			allowed := make([]string, 0, len(t)-1)
742			for _, tree := range engine.trees {
743				if tree.method == httpMethod {
744					continue
745				}
746				if value := tree.root.getValue(rPath, nil, c.skippedNodes, unescape); value.handlers != nil {
747					allowed = append(allowed, tree.method)
748				}
749			}
750			if len(allowed) > 0 {
751				c.handlers = engine.allNoMethod
752				c.writermem.Header().Set("Allow", strings.Join(allowed, ", "))
753				serveError(c, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed, default405Body)
754				return
755			}
756		}
757	
758		c.handlers = engine.allNoRoute
759		serveError(c, http.StatusNotFound, default404Body)
760	}
761	
762	var mimePlain = []string{MIMEPlain}
763	
764	func serveError(c *Context, code int, defaultMessage []byte) {
765		c.writermem.status = code
766		c.Next()
767		if c.writermem.Written() {
768			return
769		}
770		if c.writermem.Status() == code {
771			c.writermem.Header()["Content-Type"] = mimePlain
772			_, err := c.Writer.Write(defaultMessage)
773			if err != nil {
774				debugPrint("cannot write message to writer during serve error: %v", err)
775			}
776			return
777		}
778		c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
779	}
780	
781	func redirectTrailingSlash(c *Context) {
782		req := c.Request
783		p := req.URL.Path
784		if prefix := path.Clean(c.Request.Header.Get("X-Forwarded-Prefix")); prefix != "." {
785			prefix = sanitizePathChars(prefix)
786			prefix = removeRepeatedChar(prefix, '/')
787	
788			p = prefix + "/" + req.URL.Path
789		}
790		req.URL.Path = p + "/"
791		if length := len(p); length > 1 && p[length-1] == '/' {
792			req.URL.Path = p[:length-1]
793		}
794		redirectRequest(c)
795	}
796	
797	// sanitizePathChars removes unsafe characters from path strings,
798	// keeping only ASCII letters, ASCII numbers, forward slashes, and hyphens.
799	func sanitizePathChars(s string) string {
800		return strings.Map(func(r rune) rune {
801			if (r >= 'a' && r <= 'z') || (r >= 'A' && r <= 'Z') || (r >= '0' && r <= '9') || r == '/' || r == '-' {
802				return r
803			}
804			return -1
805		}, s)
806	}

808	func redirectFixedPath(c *Context, root *node, trailingSlash bool) bool {
809		req := c.Request
810		rPath := req.URL.Path
811	
812		if fixedPath, ok := root.findCaseInsensitivePath(cleanPath(rPath), trailingSlash); ok {
813			req.URL.Path = bytesconv.BytesToString(fixedPath)
814			redirectRequest(c)
815			return true
816		}
817		return false
818	}

820	func redirectRequest(c *Context) {
821		req := c.Request
822		rPath := req.URL.Path
823		rURL := req.URL.String()
824	
825		code := http.StatusMovedPermanently // Permanent redirect, request with GET method
826		if req.Method != http.MethodGet {
827			code = http.StatusTemporaryRedirect
828		}
829		debugPrint("redirecting request %d: %s --> %s", code, rPath, rURL)
830		http.Redirect(c.Writer, req, rURL, code)
831		c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
832	}

12	)
13	
14	var (
15		// regEnLetter matches english letters for http method name
16		regEnLetter = regexp.MustCompile("^[A-Z]+$")
17	
18		// anyMethods for RouterGroup Any method
19		anyMethods = []string{
20			http.MethodGet, http.MethodPost, http.MethodPut, http.MethodPatch,
21			http.MethodHead, http.MethodOptions, http.MethodDelete, http.MethodConnect,
22			http.MethodTrace,
23		}
24	)
25	
26	// IRouter defines all router handle interface includes single and group router.
27	type IRouter interface {
28		IRoutes
29		Group(string, ...HandlerFunc) *RouterGroup
30	}
31	
32	// IRoutes defines all router handle interface.
33	type IRoutes interface {
34		Use(...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
35	
36		Handle(string, string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
37		Any(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
38		GET(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
39		POST(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
40		DELETE(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
41		PATCH(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
42		PUT(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
43		OPTIONS(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
44		HEAD(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
45		Match([]string, string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
46	
47		StaticFile(string, string) IRoutes
48		StaticFileFS(string, string, http.FileSystem) IRoutes
49		Static(string, string) IRoutes
50		StaticFS(string, http.FileSystem) IRoutes
51	}
52	
53	// RouterGroup is used internally to configure router, a RouterGroup is associated with
54	// a prefix and an array of handlers (middleware).
55	type RouterGroup struct {
56		Handlers HandlersChain
57		basePath string
58		engine   *Engine
59		root     bool
60	}
61	
62	var _ IRouter = (*RouterGroup)(nil)
63	
64	// Use adds middleware to the group, see example code in GitHub.
65	func (group *RouterGroup) Use(middleware ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
66		group.Handlers = append(group.Handlers, middleware...)
67		return group.returnObj()
68	}
69	
70	// Group creates a new router group. You should add all the routes that have common middlewares or the same path prefix.
71	// For example, all the routes that use a common middleware for authorization could be grouped.
72	func (group *RouterGroup) Group(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) *RouterGroup {
73		return &RouterGroup{
74			Handlers: group.combineHandlers(handlers),
75			basePath: group.calculateAbsolutePath(relativePath),
76			engine:   group.engine,
77		}
78	}
79	
80	// BasePath returns the base path of router group.
81	// For example, if v := router.Group("/rest/n/v1/api"), v.BasePath() is "/rest/n/v1/api".
82	func (group *RouterGroup) BasePath() string {
83		return group.basePath
84	}

86	func (group *RouterGroup) handle(httpMethod, relativePath string, handlers HandlersChain) IRoutes {
87		absolutePath := group.calculateAbsolutePath(relativePath)
88		handlers = group.combineHandlers(handlers)
89		group.engine.addRoute(httpMethod, absolutePath, handlers)
90		return group.returnObj()
91	}

93	// Handle registers a new request handle and middleware with the given path and method.
94	// The last handler should be the real handler, the other ones should be middleware that can and should be shared among different routes.
95	// See the example code in GitHub.
96	//
97	// For GET, POST, PUT, PATCH and DELETE requests the respective shortcut
98	// functions can be used.
99	//
100	// This function is intended for bulk loading and to allow the usage of less
101	// frequently used, non-standardized or custom methods (e.g. for internal
102	// communication with a proxy).
103	func (group *RouterGroup) Handle(httpMethod, relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
104		if matched := regEnLetter.MatchString(httpMethod); !matched {
105			panic("http method " + httpMethod + " is not valid")
106		}
107		return group.handle(httpMethod, relativePath, handlers)
108	}

112		return group.handle(http.MethodPost, relativePath, handlers)
113	}

117		return group.handle(http.MethodGet, relativePath, handlers)
118	}

122		return group.handle(http.MethodDelete, relativePath, handlers)
123	}

128	}

132		return group.handle(http.MethodPut, relativePath, handlers)
133	}
134	
135	// OPTIONS is a shortcut for router.Handle("OPTIONS", path, handlers).
136	func (group *RouterGroup) OPTIONS(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {

138	}
139	
140	// HEAD is a shortcut for router.Handle("HEAD", path, handlers).

142		return group.handle(http.MethodHead, relativePath, handlers)
143	}
144	
145	// Any registers a route that matches all the HTTP methods.
146	// GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS, DELETE, CONNECT, TRACE.
147	func (group *RouterGroup) Any(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
148		for _, method := range anyMethods {
149			group.handle(method, relativePath, handlers)
150		}

152		return group.returnObj()
153	}
154	
155	// Match registers a route that matches the specified methods that you declared.
156	func (group *RouterGroup) Match(methods []string, relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
157		for _, method := range methods {
158			group.handle(method, relativePath, handlers)
159		}
160	
161		return group.returnObj()
162	}

164	// StaticFile registers a single route in order to serve a single file of the local filesystem.
165	// router.StaticFile("favicon.ico", "./resources/favicon.ico")
166	func (group *RouterGroup) StaticFile(relativePath, filepath string) IRoutes {
167		return group.staticFileHandler(relativePath, func(c *Context) {

173	// router.StaticFileFS("favicon.ico", "./resources/favicon.ico", Dir{".", false})
174	// Gin by default uses: gin.Dir()
175	func (group *RouterGroup) StaticFileFS(relativePath, filepath string, fs http.FileSystem) IRoutes {
176		return group.staticFileHandler(relativePath, func(c *Context) {
177			c.FileFromFS(filepath, fs)
178		})
179	}

181	func (group *RouterGroup) staticFileHandler(relativePath string, handler HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
182		if strings.Contains(relativePath, ":") || strings.Contains(relativePath, "*") {
183			panic("URL parameters can not be used when serving a static file")
184		}
185		group.GET(relativePath, handler)
186		group.HEAD(relativePath, handler)
187		return group.returnObj()
188	}
189	
190	// Static serves files from the given file system root.
191	// Internally a http.FileServer is used, therefore http.NotFound is used instead
192	// of the Router's NotFound handler.
193	// To use the operating system's file system implementation,
194	// use :
195	//
196	//	router.Static("/static", "/var/www")
197	func (group *RouterGroup) Static(relativePath, root string) IRoutes {
198		return group.StaticFS(relativePath, Dir(root, false))
199	}
200	
201	// StaticFS works just like `Static()` but a custom `http.FileSystem` can be used instead.
202	// Gin by default uses: gin.Dir()
203	func (group *RouterGroup) StaticFS(relativePath string, fs http.FileSystem) IRoutes {
204		if strings.Contains(relativePath, ":") || strings.Contains(relativePath, "*") {
205			panic("URL parameters can not be used when serving a static folder")
206		}
207		handler := group.createStaticHandler(relativePath, fs)
208		urlPattern := path.Join(relativePath, "/*filepath")
209	
210		// Register GET and HEAD handlers
211		group.GET(urlPattern, handler)
212		group.HEAD(urlPattern, handler)
213		return group.returnObj()
214	}
215	
216	func (group *RouterGroup) createStaticHandler(relativePath string, fs http.FileSystem) HandlerFunc {
217		absolutePath := group.calculateAbsolutePath(relativePath)
218		fileServer := http.StripPrefix(absolutePath, http.FileServer(fs))
219	
220		return func(c *Context) {
221			if _, noListing := fs.(*OnlyFilesFS); noListing {
222				c.Writer.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
223			}
224	
225			file := c.Param("filepath")
226			// Check if file exists and/or if we have permission to access it
227			f, err := fs.Open(file)
228			if err != nil {
229				c.Writer.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
230				c.handlers = group.engine.noRoute
231				// Reset index
232				c.index = -1
233				return
234			}
235			f.Close()
236	
237			fileServer.ServeHTTP(c.Writer, c.Request)
238		}
239	}
240	
241	func (group *RouterGroup) combineHandlers(handlers HandlersChain) HandlersChain {
242		finalSize := len(group.Handlers) + len(handlers)
243		assert1(finalSize < int(abortIndex), "too many handlers")
244		mergedHandlers := make(HandlersChain, finalSize)
245		copy(mergedHandlers, group.Handlers)
246		copy(mergedHandlers[len(group.Handlers):], handlers)
247		return mergedHandlers
248	}
249	
250	func (group *RouterGroup) calculateAbsolutePath(relativePath string) string {
251		return joinPaths(group.basePath, relativePath)
252	}
253	
254	func (group *RouterGroup) returnObj() IRoutes {
255		if group.root {
256			return group.engine
257		}
258		return group
259	}

74			n.children = append(n.children[:len(n.children)-1], child, wildcardChild)
75		} else {

90	type nodeType uint8

110	// Increments priority of the given child and reorders if necessary
111	func (n *node) incrementChildPrio(pos int) int {
112		cs := n.children
113		cs[pos].priority++
114		prio := cs[pos].priority
115	
116		// Adjust position (move to front)
117		newPos := pos
118		for ; newPos > 0 && cs[newPos-1].priority < prio; newPos-- {
119			// Swap node positions
120			cs[newPos-1], cs[newPos] = cs[newPos], cs[newPos-1]
121		}

123		// Build new index char string
124		if newPos != pos {

126				n.indices[pos:pos+1] + // The index char we move

162					children:  n.children,
163					handlers:  n.handlers,
164					priority:  n.priority - 1,

11	// Content-Type MIME of the most common data formats.
12	const (
13		MIMEJSON              = "application/json"
14		MIMEHTML              = "text/html"
15		MIMEXML               = "application/xml"
16		MIMEXML2              = "text/xml"
17		MIMEPlain             = "text/plain"
18		MIMEPOSTForm          = "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
19		MIMEMultipartPOSTForm = "multipart/form-data"
20		MIMEPROTOBUF          = "application/x-protobuf"
21		MIMEMSGPACK           = "application/x-msgpack"
22		MIMEMSGPACK2          = "application/msgpack"
23		MIMEYAML              = "application/x-yaml"
24		MIMEYAML2             = "application/yaml"
25		MIMETOML              = "application/toml"
26		MIMEBSON              = "application/bson"
27	)
28	
29	// Binding describes the interface which needs to be implemented for binding the
30	// data present in the request such as JSON request body, query parameters or
31	// the form POST.
32	type Binding interface {
33		Name() string
34		Bind(*http.Request, any) error
35	}
36	
37	// BindingBody adds BindBody method to Binding. BindBody is similar with Bind,
38	// but it reads the body from supplied bytes instead of req.Body.
39	type BindingBody interface {
40		Binding
41		BindBody([]byte, any) error
42	}
43	
44	// BindingUri adds BindUri method to Binding. BindUri is similar with Bind,
45	// but it reads the Params.
46	type BindingUri interface {
47		Name() string
48		BindUri(map[string][]string, any) error
49	}
50	
51	// StructValidator is the minimal interface which needs to be implemented in
52	// order for it to be used as the validator engine for ensuring the correctness
53	// of the request. Gin provides a default implementation for this using
54	// https://github.com/go-playground/validator/tree/v10.6.1.
55	type StructValidator interface {
56		// ValidateStruct can receive any kind of type and it should never panic, even if the configuration is not right.
57		// If the received type is a slice|array, the validation should be performed travel on every element.
58		// If the received type is not a struct or slice|array, any validation should be skipped and nil must be returned.
59		// If the received type is a struct or pointer to a struct, the validation should be performed.
60		// If the struct is not valid or the validation itself fails, a descriptive error should be returned.
61		// Otherwise nil must be returned.
62		ValidateStruct(any) error
63	
64		// Engine returns the underlying validator engine which powers the
65		// StructValidator implementation.
66		Engine() any
67	}
68	
69	// Validator is the default validator which implements the StructValidator
70	// interface. It uses https://github.com/go-playground/validator/tree/v10.6.1
71	// under the hood.
72	var Validator StructValidator = &defaultValidator{}

74	// These implement the Binding interface and can be used to bind the data
75	// present in the request to struct instances.

77		JSON          BindingBody = jsonBinding{}
78		XML           BindingBody = xmlBinding{}
79		Form          Binding     = formBinding{}
80		Query         Binding     = queryBinding{}
81		FormPost      Binding     = formPostBinding{}
82		FormMultipart Binding     = formMultipartBinding{}
83		ProtoBuf      BindingBody = protobufBinding{}
84		MsgPack       BindingBody = msgpackBinding{}
85		YAML          BindingBody = yamlBinding{}
86		Uri           BindingUri  = uriBinding{}
87		Header        Binding     = headerBinding{}
88		Plain         BindingBody = plainBinding{}
89		TOML          BindingBody = tomlBinding{}
90		BSON          BindingBody = bsonBinding{}
91	)

93	// Default returns the appropriate Binding instance based on the HTTP method
94	// and the content type.
95	func Default(method, contentType string) Binding {
96		if method == http.MethodGet {
97			return Form
98		}
99	
100		switch contentType {
101		case MIMEJSON:
102			return JSON
103		case MIMEXML, MIMEXML2:
104			return XML
105		case MIMEPROTOBUF:
106			return ProtoBuf

120	}

123		if Validator == nil {
124			return nil
125		}
126		return Validator.ValidateStruct(obj)
127	}

2	// Based on the path package, Copyright 2009 The Go Authors.

4	// at https://github.com/julienschmidt/httprouter/blob/master/LICENSE.
5	
6	package gin
7	
8	const stackBufSize = 128
9	
10	// cleanPath is the URL version of path.Clean, it returns a canonical URL path
11	// for p, eliminating . and .. elements.
12	//
13	// The following rules are applied iteratively until no further processing can
14	// be done:
15	//  1. Replace multiple slashes with a single slash.
16	//  2. Eliminate each . path name element (the current directory).
17	//  3. Eliminate each inner .. path name element (the parent directory)
18	//     along with the non-.. element that precedes it.
19	//  4. Eliminate .. elements that begin a rooted path:
20	//     that is, replace "/.." by "/" at the beginning of a path.
21	//
22	// If the result of this process is an empty string, "/" is returned.
23	func cleanPath(p string) string {
24		// Turn empty string into "/"
25		if p == "" {
26			return "/"
27		}
28	
29		// Reasonably sized buffer on stack to avoid allocations in the common case.
30		// If a larger buffer is required, it gets allocated dynamically.
31		buf := make([]byte, 0, stackBufSize)
32	
33		n := len(p)
34	
35		// Invariants:
36		//      reading from path; r is index of next byte to process.
37		//      writing to buf; w is index of next byte to write.
38	
39		// path must start with '/'
40		r := 1
41		w := 1
42	
43		if p[0] != '/' {
44			r = 0
45	
46			if n+1 > stackBufSize {
47				buf = make([]byte, n+1)
48			} else {
49				buf = buf[:n+1]
50			}
51			buf[0] = '/'
52		}
53	
54		trailing := n > 1 && p[n-1] == '/'
55	
56		// A bit more clunky without a 'lazybuf' like the path package, but the loop
57		// gets completely inlined (bufApp calls).
58		// loop has no expensive function calls (except 1x make)		// So in contrast to the path package this loop has no expensive function
59		// calls (except make, if needed).
60	
61		for r < n {
62			switch {
63			case p[r] == '/':
64				// empty path element, trailing slash is added after the end
65				r++
66	
67			case p[r] == '.' && r+1 == n:
68				trailing = true
69				r++
70	
71			case p[r] == '.' && p[r+1] == '/':
72				// . element
73				r += 2
74	
75			case p[r] == '.' && p[r+1] == '.' && (r+2 == n || p[r+2] == '/'):
76				// .. element: remove to last /
77				r += 3
78	
79				if w > 1 {
80					// can backtrack
81					w--
82	
83					if len(buf) == 0 {
84						for w > 1 && p[w] != '/' {
85							w--
86						}
87					} else {
88						for w > 1 && buf[w] != '/' {
89							w--
90						}
91					}
92				}
93	
94			default:
95				// Real path element.
96				// Add slash if needed
97				if w > 1 {
98					bufApp(&buf, p, w, '/')
99					w++
100				}
101	
102				// Copy element
103				for r < n && p[r] != '/' {
104					bufApp(&buf, p, w, p[r])
105					w++
106					r++
107				}
108			}
109		}
110	
111		// Re-append trailing slash
112		if trailing && w > 1 {
113			bufApp(&buf, p, w, '/')
114			w++
115		}
116	
117		// If the original string was not modified (or only shortened at the end),
118		// return the respective substring of the original string.
119		// Otherwise return a new string from the buffer.
120		if len(buf) == 0 {
121			return p[:w]
122		}
123		return string(buf[:w])
124	}
125	
126	// Internal helper to lazily create a buffer if necessary.
127	// Calls to this function get inlined.
128	func bufApp(buf *[]byte, s string, w int, c byte) {
129		b := *buf
130		if len(b) == 0 {
131			// No modification of the original string so far.
132			// If the next character is the same as in the original string, we do
133			// not yet have to allocate a buffer.
134			if s[w] == c {
135				return
136			}
137	
138			// Otherwise use either the stack buffer, if it is large enough, or
139			// allocate a new buffer on the heap, and copy all previous characters.
140			length := len(s)
141			if length > cap(b) {
142				*buf = make([]byte, length)
143			} else {
144				*buf = (*buf)[:length]
145			}
146			b = *buf
147	
148			copy(b, s[:w])
149		}
150		b[w] = c
151	}
152	
153	// removeRepeatedChar removes multiple consecutive 'char's from a string.
154	// if s == "/a//b///c////" && char == '/', it returns "/a/b/c/"
155	func removeRepeatedChar(s string, char byte) string {
156		// Check if there are any consecutive chars
157		hasRepeatedChar := false
158		for i := 1; i < len(s); i++ {
159			if s[i] == char && s[i-1] == char {
160				hasRepeatedChar = true
161				break
162			}
163		}
164		if !hasRepeatedChar {
165			return s
166		}
167	
168		// Reasonably sized buffer on stack to avoid allocations in the common case.
169		buf := make([]byte, 0, stackBufSize)
170	
171		// Invariants:
172		//      reading from s; r is index of next byte to process.
173		//      writing to buf; w is index of next byte to write.
174		r := 0
175		w := 0
176	
177		for n := len(s); r < n; {
178			if s[r] == char {
179				// Write the first char
180				bufApp(&buf, s, w, char)
181				w++
182				r++
183	
184				// Skip all consecutive chars
185				for r < n && s[r] == char {
186					r++
187				}
188			} else {
189				// Copy non-char character
190				bufApp(&buf, s, w, s[r])
191				w++
192				r++
193			}
194		}
195	
196		// If the original string was not modified (or only shortened at the end),
197		// return the respective substring of the original string.
198		// Otherwise, return a new string from the buffer.
199		if len(buf) == 0 {
200			return s[:w]
201		}
202		return string(buf[:w])
203	}

**Supporting Files:**

```go
    // Routing configuration:
    RedirectTrailingSlash   bool  // Auto-redirect /foo → /foo/
    RedirectFixedPath       bool  // Case-insensitive path correction
    HandleMethodNotAllowed  bool  // Return 405 for unallowed methods
    
    // Client IP detection:
    ForwardedByClientIP     bool     // Parse client IP from headers
    RemoteIPHeaders         []string // Header names to check (default: X-Forwarded-For, X-Real-IP)
    TrustedPlatform         string   // Platform-specific IP headers (Google App Engine, Cloudflare, etc.)

    UseEscapedPath          bool  // Use URL.EscapedPath() (overrides UseRawPath)
    UnescapePathValues      bool  // Unescape path parameter values
    RemoveExtraSlash        bool  // Allow parameters with extra slashes
```

```go
func (engine *Engine) handleHTTPRequest(c *Context) {
    httpMethod := c.Request.Method
    rPath := c.Request.URL.Path  // Get request path
    
    // Handle path encoding (escaped, raw, or standard)
    if engine.UseEscapedPath {
        rPath = c.Request.URL.EscapedPath()
    } else if engine.UseRawPath && len(c.Request.URL.RawPath) > 0 {
        rPath = c.Request.URL.RawPath
    }
    
    // Optional: clean path (remove ../ and //)
    if engine.RemoveExtraSlash {
        rPath = cleanPath(rPath)
    }
    
    // Find route tree for HTTP method
    t := engine.trees
    for i, tl := 0, len(t); i < tl; i++ {
        if t[i].method != httpMethod {
            continue
        }
        root := t[i].root
        
        // Match route in radix tree
        value := root.getValue(rPath, c.params, c.skippedNodes, unescape)
        
        if value.handlers != nil {
            // Route found
            c.Params = *value.params
            c.handlers = value.handlers
            c.fullPath = value.fullPath
            c.Next()  // Execute handler chain
            c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
            return
        }
        
        // Handle redirects for trailing slashes
        if httpMethod != http.MethodConnect && rPath != "/" {
            if value.tsr && engine.RedirectTrailingSlash {
                redirectTrailingSlash(c)
                return
            }
            if engine.RedirectFixedPath && redirectFixedPath(c, root, engine.RedirectFixedPath) {
                return
            }
        }
        break
    }
    
    // Handle 405 Method Not Allowed
    if engine.HandleMethodNotAllowed && len(t) > 0 {
        allowed := []string{}
        for _, tree := range engine.trees {
            if tree.method == httpMethod { continue }
            if value := tree.root.getValue(rPath, nil, c.skippedNodes, unescape); value.handlers != nil {
                allowed = append(allowed, tree.method)
            }
        }
        if len(allowed) > 0 {
            c.handlers = engine.allNoMethod
            c.writermem.Header().Set("Allow", strings.Join(allowed, ", "))
            serveError(c, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed, default405Body)
            return
        }
    }
    
    // Handle 404 Not Found
    c.handlers = engine.allNoRoute
    serveError(c, http.StatusNotFound, default404Body)
}
```

```go
func (c *Context) Render(code int, r render.Render) {
    c.Status(code)                    // Set HTTP status via ResponseWriter.WriteHeader()
    
    if !bodyAllowedForStatus(code) {  // 204, 304, etc. can't have body
        r.WriteContentType(c.Writer)
        c.Writer.WriteHeaderNow()     // Force header write
        return
    }
    
    if err := r.Render(c.Writer); err != nil {  // Render to response
        _ = c.Error(err)              // Add error to context
        c.Abort()                     // Stop further processing
    }
}
```

```go
func (engine *Engine) handleHTTPRequest(c *Context) {

    rPath := c.Request.URL.Path  // Get request path

    if engine.UseEscapedPath {
        rPath = c.Request.URL.EscapedPath()
    } else if engine.UseRawPath && len(c.Request.URL.RawPath) > 0 {
        rPath = c.Request.URL.RawPath
    }
    
    // Optional: clean path (remove ../ and //)
    if engine.RemoveExtraSlash {
        rPath = cleanPath(rPath)
    }
    
    // Find route tree for HTTP method
    t := engine.trees
    for i, tl := 0, len(t); i < tl; i++ {
        if t[i].method != httpMethod {
            continue
        }
        root := t[i].root
        
        // Match route in radix tree
        value := root.getValue(rPath, c.params, c.skippedNodes, unescape)
        
        if value.handlers != nil {
            // Route found
            c.Params = *value.params
            c.handlers = value.handlers
            c.fullPath = value.fullPath
            c.Next()  // Execute handler chain
            c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
            return
        }
        
        // Handle redirects for trailing slashes
        if httpMethod != http.MethodConnect && rPath != "/" {
            if value.tsr && engine.RedirectTrailingSlash {
                redirectTrailingSlash(c)
                return
            }
            if engine.RedirectFixedPath && redirectFixedPath(c, root, engine.RedirectFixedPath) {
                return
            }
        }
        break

    if engine.HandleMethodNotAllowed && len(t) > 0 {
        allowed := []string{}
        for _, tree := range engine.trees {
            if tree.method == httpMethod { continue }
            if value := tree.root.getValue(rPath, nil, c.skippedNodes, unescape); value.handlers != nil {
                allowed = append(allowed, tree.method)
            }
        }
        if len(allowed) > 0 {
            c.handlers = engine.allNoMethod
            c.writermem.Header().Set("Allow", strings.Join(allowed, ", "))
            serveError(c, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed, default405Body)
            return
        }
    }
    
    // Handle 404 Not Found
    c.handlers = engine.allNoRoute
    serveError(c, http.StatusNotFound, default404Body)
```

This is the **gin-gonic/gin** Go HTTP framework. Here's a thorough breakdown of its components and data flow:

---

## Core Components

### 1. `Engine` — `gin.go:92`
The central hub. Embeds `RouterGroup` to inherit routing methods, owns the per-method radix tree forest, holds a `sync.Pool` of `Context` objects, and stores global configuration.

Key fields:
- `trees methodTrees` — slice of `methodTree`, one per HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.)
- `pool sync.Pool` — pools `*Context` objects to avoid GC pressure
- `allNoRoute / allNoMethod HandlersChain` — pre-built 404/405 handler chains (global middleware prepended)
- `RouterGroup` (embedded) — provides `Use()`, `GET()`, `Group()`, etc.

### 2. `RouterGroup` — `routergroup.go:55`
Hierarchical grouping. Each group carries a `basePath` prefix and a `Handlers HandlersChain` (its accumulated middleware). Sub-groups inherit and extend both.

```go
type RouterGroup struct {
    Handlers HandlersChain  // middleware for this group
    basePath string
    engine   *Engine
    root     bool
}
```

`RouterGroup.handle()` at `routergroup.go:86` is the single code path for all route registrations — it calls `combineHandlers()` (line 241) to merge group middleware with the route's own handlers, then `engine.addRoute()` to insert into the radix tree.

### 3. `Context` — `context.go:61`
Per-request state object. Pooled, not allocated per request. Holds everything a handler needs:
- `Request *http.Request` / `Writer ResponseWriter`
- `Params` — matched URL parameters (`:id`, `*filepath`)
- `handlers HandlersChain` — the full chain for this request
- `index int8` — cursor into the chain, drives `Next()` / `Abort()`
- `Keys map[any]any` — scoped key-value storage for passing data between middleware
- `queryCache / formCache` — lazy-parsed query/form data

### 4. `HandlersChain` — `gin.go:57`
```go
type HandlersChain []HandlerFunc   // []func(*Context)
```
Global middleware + group middleware + route handlers are all flattened into one slice per route. No distinction at runtime.

### 5. Radix Trie (`node`) — `tree.go:99`
One tree per HTTP method. Supports four node types:
- `static` — literal path segment
- `root` — tree root
- `param` — named parameter (`:id`)
- `catchAll` — wildcard (`*filepath`)

Route insertion is `addRoute()` at `tree.go`; matching is `getValue()` at `tree.go:418`.

### 6. `render.Render` interface — `render/render.go:9`
```go
type Render interface {
    Render(http.ResponseWriter) error
    WriteContentType(w http.ResponseWriter)
}
```
Implementations: `JSON`, `XML`, `HTML`, `YAML`, `TOML`, `Data`, `Reader`, `Redirect`, `String`, etc. in the `render/` package.

### 7. `ResponseWriter` — `response_writer.go:23`
Wraps `http.ResponseWriter`, tracks `status int` and `size int` (bytes written). Exposes `WriteHeaderNow()` to force header flush. Embedded in `Context.writermem`.

---

## Data Flow: Entry Point → Response

```
net/http.Server.ListenAndServe()
       │
       ▼
Engine.ServeHTTP(w, req)                        gin.go:662
   │  1. engine.routeTreesUpdated.Do(updateRouteTrees)  — once-per-lifetime tree finalization
   │  2. c := engine.pool.Get().(*Context)              — reuse pooled context
   │  3. c.writermem.reset(w) ; c.Request = req ; c.reset()
   │
   ▼
Engine.handleHTTPRequest(c)                     gin.go:690
   │  1. Extract method and path (UseRawPath / UseEscapedPath / RemoveExtraSlash options)
   │  2. Linear scan engine.trees for matching HTTP method
   │  3. value := root.getValue(rPath, c.params, c.skippedNodes, unescape)
   │
   ▼
node.getValue(path, ...)                        tree.go:418
   │  • Binary-search `node.indices` to find matching child
   │  • For :param nodes — capture value up to next '/'        (tree.go:487-521)
   │  • For *catchAll nodes — capture rest of path             (tree.go:549-579)
   │  • TSR check (trailing-slash redirect flag)
   │  • Backtrack via skippedNodes if needed                   (tree.go:591-660)
   │  Returns: nodeValue{handlers, params, fullPath, tsr}
   │
   ▼
handleHTTPRequest (continued)
   │  c.Params    = value.params
   │  c.handlers  = value.handlers   (global middleware + group middleware + route handler)
   │  c.fullPath  = value.fullPath
   │  c.Next()                                               ← starts chain
   │
   ▼
Context.Next()                                  context.go:188
   │  for c.index < len(c.handlers) {
   │      c.handlers[c.index](c)      ← calls each HandlerFunc
   │      c.index++
   │  }
   │
   │  Middleware calls c.Next() to proceed; c.Abort() sets index=AbortIndex to stop.
   │
   ▼
Route Handler (user code)
   │  e.g., c.JSON(200, gin.H{"id": c.Param("id")})
   │
   ▼
Context.JSON / Context.Render                   context.go:1152
   │  c.Status(code)          — sets status on writermem
   │  render.JSON{Data: obj}.Render(c.Writer)
   │       → json.Marshal(obj) → w.Write(jsonBytes)
   │
   ▼
handleHTTPRequest (after Next returns)
   │  c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()    — flushes status + headers if not yet sent
   │
   ▼
ServeHTTP
   engine.pool.Put(c)                 — return Context to pool
```

---

## Supporting Packages

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `binding/` | Decodes request bodies/query/form into structs; validation |
| `render/` | Pluggable response encoders (JSON, XML, HTML templates, …) |
| `codec/json/` | Swappable JSON backend (stdlib, sonic, jsoniter) |
| `internal/bytesconv/` | Zero-alloc string↔byte conversions |
| `internal/fs/` | Custom `FileSystem` wrapper for static file serving |

---

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Context pooling** (`sync.Pool`) — avoids one allocation per request; this is why `Context` is reset on every reuse (`c.reset()` at `context.go`).
2. **Flat `HandlersChain`** — middleware and handlers are indistinguishable at runtime; the cursor `c.index` drives execution. This lets middleware wrap handlers naturally by calling `c.Next()` before/after logic.
3. **Per-method radix trees** — route lookup is O(path length), not O(route count). Separate trees per method mean method dispatch costs only a linear scan over ~9 entries.
4. **`RouterGroup` embedding** — `Engine` *is* a `RouterGroup`, so top-level `router.GET(...)` and `router.Group(...)` work without a separate "root group" concept, though `root bool` in `RouterGroup` tracks it internally.

3. **Per-method radix trees** — route lookup is O(path length), not O(route count). Separate trees per method mean method dispatch costs only a linear scan over ~9 entries.
4. **`RouterGroup` embedding** — `Engine` *is* a `RouterGroup`, so top-level `router.GET(...)` and `router.Group(...)` work without a separate "root group" concept, though `root bool` in `RouterGroup` tracks it internally.
